     The Numbers Business Report

Mortal Engines (2018)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $15,951,040Details
International Box Office $69,530,864
Worldwide Box Office $85,481,904
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $4,855,958
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $6,428,731
Total $11,284,689
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Thousands of years after civilization was destroyed by a cataclysmic event, humankind has adapted and a new way of living has evolved. Gigantic moving cities now roam the Earth, ruthlessly preying upon smaller traction towns. Tom Natsworthy—who hails from a Lower Tier of the great traction city of London—finds himself fighting for his own survival after he encounters the dangerous fugitive Hester Shaw. Two opposites, whose paths should never have crossed, forge an unlikely alliance that is destined to change the course of the future.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$7,559,850 (47.4% of total gross)
Legs:2.11 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:18.7% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$100,000,000 (worldwide box office is 0.9 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,103 opening theaters/3,103 max. theaters, 3.2 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $20,933,636

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: December 14th, 2018 (Wide) by Universal
December 14th, 2018 (IMAX) by Universal
International Releases: December 5th, 2018 (Wide) (South Korea)
December 5th, 2018 (Wide) (Philippines)
December 5th, 2018 (Wide) (Indonesia)
December 5th, 2018 (Wide) (Egypt)
December 5th, 2018 (Wide) (Belgium)
Video Release: February 19th, 2019 by Universal Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for sequences of futuristic violence and action.
(Rating bulletin 2550 (Cert #51767), 10/24/2018)
Running Time: 128 minutes
Film format: 3-D, 3-D - Post-production Conversion, IMAX: DMR
Geographical setting: London
Plot point: Exile, Revenge, Young Child Dealing with the Death of a Parent
Social setting: Post Apocalypse
Subgenre: Action Adventure
Time period setting: Steampunk
Source:Based on Fiction Book/Short Story
Genre:Action
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Science Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Media Rights Capital, Wingnut Films, Universal Pictures, Scholastic Entertainment Inc., Silvertongue Films, Perfect World Pictures
Production Countries: New Zealand, United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Thousands of years after civilization was destroyed by a cataclysmic event, humankind has adapted and a new way of living has evolved. Gigantic moving cities now roam the Earth, ruthlessly preying upon smaller traction towns. Tom Natsworthy—who hails from a Lower Tier of the great traction city of London—finds himself fighting for his own survival after he encounters the dangerous fugitive Hester Shaw. Two opposites, whose paths should never have crossed, forge an unlikely alliance that is destined to change the course of the future.
